title: Lines Written In "Letters Of An Italian Nun And An  English Gentleman, By J. J. Rousseau;[1]Founded On Facts."
content:
"Away, away, - your flattering arts
    May now betray some simpler hearts;
    And you will smile at their believing,
    And they shall weep at your deceiving."
meaning:
This evocative piece by George Gordon Byron, titled "Lines Written In "Letters Of An Italian Nun And An  English Gentleman, By J. J. Rousseau;[1]Founded On Facts."", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
